steps.note: When +request fires successfully, jobHooks.emit('job:created', job) is called with the full IJob object. Listeners receive the new job before the confirmation message is sent.

steps.cmd: +request Hook Test=Testing that hook events fire in the right order.
steps.label: fires job:created

steps.note: jobHooks.emit('job:commented', job, comment) fires after the comment is persisted. The second argument is the IJobComment that was just appended.
Both +job/claim and +job/assign emit job:assigned after updating assignedTo and assigneeName.

steps.sub: job:commented — Staff Adds a Public Comment

steps.note: Staff +job/comment also emits job:commented. staffOnly is false so the player can see it.

steps.sub: job:commented — Staff Adds a Staff-Only Note

steps.note: Staff +job/staffnote emits job:commented with staffOnly: true. Listeners can decide whether to propagate notifications based on this flag.
jobHooks.emit('job:priority-changed', job, oldPriority) passes both the updated job and the previous priority level.
jobHooks.emit('job:closed', job) fires after the job is moved to the archive. The job object has status 'closed'. Submitter receives mail independently of the hook.
+job/resolve emits job:resolved instead of job:closed, letting listeners distinguish 'completed work' from 'admin closure'. The IJob has status 'resolved'.

steps.sub: job:reopened — Staff Reopens an Archived Job

steps.note: jobHooks.emit('job:reopened', job) fires after the job is moved back to active. Status is 'open' and closedByName is cleared.
jobHooks.emit('job:deleted', job) fires on +job/delete. No archive copy is created. Listeners are the only place downstream systems can react to the deletion.
